title: Today's AI Brief: Has Microsoft Lost Its Mojo (Again)?
summary: Microsoft's Build developer conference and Elon Musk's trillionaire ambitions
imageAlt: Image from source article for Has Microsoft Lost Its Mojo (Again)?

body.text: Welcome to Today's AI Brief. I'm Leo Stone, and I'm here to walk you through the latest news in the world of artificial intelligence. Let's dive right in.

First up, Microsoft's annual Build developer's conference kicked off this week, with CEO Satya Nadella touting the company's new products and AI capabilities. The focus was on 'agentic AI,' which sounds like a fancy term for AI that can actually do stuff on its own. I'm not sure if that's exciting or terrifying, but it's definitely something to keep an eye on.

Next, Elon Musk is on a roll. According to a report on The Verge, Musk is steamrolling Wall Street with his SpaceX IPO plans, which could potentially make him a trillionaire. That's a lot of zeros, and it's anyone's guess how it'll all play out.

I am looking at these two stories, and what stands out to me is the contrast between Microsoft's established presence in the AI space and Elon Musk's wild card ambitions. Microsoft has been working on AI for years, but it's still unclear if they've found their footing. Meanwhile, Elon Musk seems to be throwing caution to the wind and making bold bets on the future.

My read is that we're in for a fascinating ride as these tech giants continue to push the boundaries of what's possible with AI. From platform shifts to model economics, there's a lot to unpack here. For now, I'll just be keeping an eye on how these stories unfold.

sources.title: Elon Musk is steamrolling Wall Street to become a trillionaire
Boston Children’s uses AI to unlock new diagnoses
